title: Bridge Essay: Spontaneous Activity in Primary Visual Cortex Relates to Visual Creativity.
concept: Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Spontaneous Activity in Primary Visual Cortex Relates to Visual Creativity.** - Front Hum Neurosci (2021). DOI: 10.3389/fnhum.2021.625888. PMID: 33867956. Cognitive and neural processes underlying visual creativity have attracted substantial attention. The current research uses a critical time point analysis (CTPA) to examine how spontaneous activity in the primary visual area (PVA) is related to visual creativity. We acquired the functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data of 16 participants at the resting state and during performing a visual creative synthesis task. According to the CTPA, we then classified spontaneous activity in the PVA
